questions.question_text: How many members serve on the Vermont Real Estate Commission?
questions.explanation: The Vermont Real Estate Commission consists of 7 members: 5 licensees and 2 public members. All are appointed by the Governor.

questions.question_text: Under which state office does the Vermont Real Estate Commission operate?
questions.explanation: The Vermont Real Estate Commission operates under the Office of Professional Regulation (OPR), which falls under the Secretary of State's office.

questions.question_text: A broker asks who appoints members to the Vermont Real Estate Commission. What is the correct answer?
questions.explanation: Members of the Vermont Real Estate Commission are appointed by the Governor. The Commission includes 5 licensee members and 2 public members.

questions.question_text: Of the 7 Commission members, how many must be licensed real estate practitioners?
questions.explanation: The Vermont Real Estate Commission has 5 licensee members and 2 public members, totaling 7. The licensee majority ensures industry expertise in regulatory decisions.

questions.question_text: A consumer wants to file a complaint about a licensee. Which office receives the complaint on behalf of the Commission?
questions.explanation: Complaints are filed with the Office of Professional Regulation (OPR), which operates under the Secretary of State and supports the Commission's regulatory functions.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is a power of the Vermont Real Estate Commission?
questions.explanation: The Commission has the power to approve education providers, adopt rules, issue licenses, and delegate administrative functions to the OPR Director.

questions.question_text: The Commission wants to update its licensing procedures. What type of authority allows it to create binding requirements?
questions.explanation: The Commission has rulemaking authority, which allows it to adopt administrative rules that carry the force of law. The current administrative rules became effective December 2015.

questions.question_text: To whom may the Commission delegate routine administrative functions?
questions.explanation: The Commission may delegate routine administrative functions to the OPR Director. This allows the Commission to focus on policy, rulemaking, and disciplinary matters.

questions.question_text: What types of requirements do the Commission's administrative rules govern in Vermont?
questions.explanation: The Commission's administrative rules govern day-to-day real estate practice requirements including licensing procedures, education standards, trust account management, and agency practices.

questions.question_text: A new education provider wants to offer pre-license courses in Vermont. Who must approve this provider?
questions.explanation: The Vermont Real Estate Commission has the power to approve education providers for pre-license and continuing education courses. Providers must meet Commission standards.

questions.question_text: During an investigation, the Commission needs testimony from a reluctant witness. What power can it exercise?
distractors: Order police to detain the witness
questions.explanation: The Commission has the power to issue subpoenas to compel testimony and the production of documents during investigations and hearings.

questions.question_text: At a formal hearing, the Commission chair wants to place a witness under oath. Is this within the Commission's authority?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, the Commission can administer oaths
distractors: No, only a judge can administer oaths
No, only the Attorney General can do this
Yes, but only through a court clerk
questions.explanation: The Commission has the express authority to administer oaths during hearings and investigations. This is one of several quasi-judicial powers granted to the Commission.

questions.question_text: An investigator from OPR arrives at a brokerage to examine transaction records. When may this inspection occur?
distractors: At any time day or night
Only after written notice 30 days prior
questions.explanation: The Commission and its investigators may examine licensee records during normal business hours. Licensees must maintain records and make them available for inspection.

questions.question_text: Who may file a complaint that triggers a Commission investigation?
questions.explanation: The Commission can investigate complaints from any source. Complaints may come from consumers, other licensees, or even be initiated by the Commission itself.

questions.question_text: A licensee under investigation wants to review evidence before the hearing. Which right does Vermont law guarantee?
questions.correct_answer: Right to a hearing with counsel
distractors: Right to dismiss the complaint unilaterally
Right to seal all records permanently
questions.explanation: Respondents in Commission proceedings have the right to a formal hearing and the right to be represented by legal counsel. This ensures due process in disciplinary matters.
